natural_language: Show me all products
sql:
SELECT TOP 100
    ProductKey,
    EnglishProductName,
    Color,
    ListPrice,
    ProductLine,
    Class
FROM dbo.DimProduct
WHERE FinishedGoodsFlag = 1
ORDER BY EnglishProductName
description: Basic SELECT with no filters, showing finished goods only

natural_language: List all customers with their names and cities
sql:
SELECT TOP 100
    c.CustomerKey,
    c.FirstName + ' ' + c.LastName AS CustomerName,
    g.City,
    g.StateProvinceName,
    g.EnglishCountryRegionName
FROM dbo.DimCustomer c
INNER JOIN dbo.DimGeography g ON c.GeographyKey = g.GeographyKey
ORDER BY c.LastName, c.FirstName
description: Simple SELECT with join, excluding confidential email/phone

natural_language: Show me products that are red in color
sql:
SELECT
    ProductKey,
    EnglishProductName,
    Color,
    ListPrice,
    StandardCost
FROM dbo.DimProduct
WHERE Color = 'Red'
    AND FinishedGoodsFlag = 1
ORDER BY ListPrice DESC
description: SELECT with WHERE clause filtering by color

natural_language: What is the total sales amount?
sql:
SELECT
    SUM(SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnitsSold,
    COUNT(DISTINCT SalesOrderNumber) AS TotalOrders
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales
description: Simple SUM aggregation with multiple metrics

natural_language: How many customers do we have?
sql:
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS TotalCustomers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT GeographyKey) AS UniqueLocations
FROM dbo.DimCustomer
description: COUNT aggregation

natural_language: What is the average order quantity for internet sales?
sql:
SELECT
    AVG(OrderQuantity) AS AvgOrderQuantity,
    AVG(SalesAmount) AS AvgOrderValue,
    AVG(SalesAmount / NULLIF(OrderQuantity, 0)) AS AvgPricePerUnit
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales
description: AVG aggregation with calculated fields

natural_language: Show me all sales with product names
sql:
SELECT TOP 100
    fis.SalesOrderNumber,
    fis.SalesOrderLineNumber,
    p.EnglishProductName,
    fis.OrderQuantity,
    fis.UnitPrice,
    fis.SalesAmount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fis.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
ORDER BY fis.SalesOrderNumber, fis.SalesOrderLineNumber
description: Simple JOIN between fact and dimension

natural_language: List all orders with customer names and product names
sql:
SELECT TOP 100
    fis.SalesOrderNumber,
    c.FirstName + ' ' + c.LastName AS CustomerName,
    p.EnglishProductName,
    fis.OrderQuantity,
    fis.SalesAmount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimCustomer c ON fis.CustomerKey = c.CustomerKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fis.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
ORDER BY fis.SalesOrderNumber
description: Multiple JOINs across fact and dimensions

natural_language: Show me sales by product category
sql:
SELECT
    pc.EnglishProductCategoryName AS Category,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(fis.OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnitsSold,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fis.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductSubcategory ps ON p.ProductSubcategoryKey = ps.ProductSubcategoryKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductCategory pc ON ps.ProductCategoryKey = pc.ProductCategoryKey
GROUP BY pc.EnglishProductCategoryName
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
description: Multiple JOINs through product hierarchy with aggregation

natural_language: Show me sales from the year 2023
sql:
SELECT
    d.CalendarYear,
    d.MonthNumberOfYear,
    d.EnglishMonthName,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS MonthlyRevenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimDate d ON fis.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
WHERE d.CalendarYear = 2013
GROUP BY d.CalendarYear, d.MonthNumberOfYear, d.EnglishMonthName
ORDER BY d.MonthNumberOfYear
description: Date filtering with dimension table (using 2013 as AdventureWorks has older data)

natural_language: What were the total sales in December 2013?
sql:
SELECT
    d.CalendarYear,
    d.EnglishMonthName,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(fis.OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnits,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount,
    AVG(fis.SalesAmount) AS AvgOrderValue
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimDate d ON fis.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
WHERE d.CalendarYear = 2013
    AND d.MonthNumberOfYear = 12
GROUP BY d.CalendarYear, d.EnglishMonthName
description: Date filtering with month and year, plus aggregation

natural_language: Show me total sales by country
sql:
SELECT
    g.EnglishCountryRegionName AS Country,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.CustomerKey) AS UniqueCustomers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount,
    AVG(fis.SalesAmount) AS AvgOrderValue
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimCustomer c ON fis.CustomerKey = c.CustomerKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimGeography g ON c.GeographyKey = g.GeographyKey
GROUP BY g.EnglishCountryRegionName
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
description: GROUP BY with multiple joins through customer geography

natural_language: What is the average unit price by product category?
sql:
SELECT
    pc.EnglishProductCategoryName AS Category,
    AVG(p.ListPrice) AS AvgListPrice,
    AVG(p.StandardCost) AS AvgCost,
    AVG(p.ListPrice - p.StandardCost) AS AvgMargin,
    COUNT(*) AS ProductCount
FROM dbo.DimProduct p
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductSubcategory ps ON p.ProductSubcategoryKey = ps.ProductSubcategoryKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductCategory pc ON ps.ProductCategoryKey = pc.ProductCategoryKey
WHERE p.FinishedGoodsFlag = 1
GROUP BY pc.EnglishProductCategoryName
ORDER BY AvgListPrice DESC
description: GROUP BY with aggregation and join through product hierarchy

natural_language: Show me the top 10 products by sales amount
sql:
SELECT TOP 10
    p.EnglishProductName,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(fis.OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnitsSold,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount,
    AVG(fis.UnitPrice) AS AvgSellingPrice
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fis.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
GROUP BY p.EnglishProductName, p.ProductKey
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
description: TOP N with aggregation and sorting

natural_language: Who are the top 5 customers by total purchase amount?
sql:
SELECT TOP 5
    c.CustomerKey,
    c.FirstName + ' ' + c.LastName AS CustomerName,
    g.City,
    g.EnglishCountryRegionName AS Country,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalPurchases,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount,
    AVG(fis.SalesAmount) AS AvgOrderValue
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimCustomer c ON fis.CustomerKey = c.CustomerKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimGeography g ON c.GeographyKey = g.GeographyKey
GROUP BY c.CustomerKey, c.FirstName, c.LastName, g.City, g.EnglishCountryRegionName
ORDER BY TotalPurchases DESC
description: TOP N customers with customer dimension and geography

natural_language: Compare sales between 2012 and 2013 by quarter
sql:
SELECT
    d.CalendarYear,
    d.CalendarQuarter,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS QuarterlyRevenue,
    SUM(fis.OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnits,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimDate d ON fis.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
WHERE d.CalendarYear IN (2012, 2013)
GROUP BY d.CalendarYear, d.CalendarQuarter
ORDER BY d.CalendarYear, d.CalendarQuarter
description: Year-over-year comparison with time grouping

natural_language: Show me the profit margin by product subcategory
sql:
SELECT
    ps.EnglishProductSubcategoryName AS Subcategory,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(fis.TotalProductCost) AS TotalCost,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount - fis.TotalProductCost) AS TotalProfit,
    CAST(SUM(fis.SalesAmount - fis.TotalProductCost) / NULLIF(SUM(fis.SalesAmount), 0) * 100 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS ProfitMarginPct
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fis.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductSubcategory ps ON p.ProductSubcategoryKey = ps.ProductSubcategoryKey
GROUP BY ps.EnglishProductSubcategoryName
HAVING SUM(fis.SalesAmount) > 0
ORDER BY ProfitMarginPct DESC
description: Calculated profit margin with multiple joins and HAVING clause

natural_language: Which sales territory has the highest average order value?
sql:
SELECT
    st.SalesTerritoryRegion,
    st.SalesTerritoryCountry,
    SUM(fis.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount,
    CAST(SUM(fis.SalesAmount) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT fis.SalesOrderNumber), 0) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS AvgOrderValue
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales fis
INNER JOIN dbo.DimSalesTerritory st ON fis.SalesTerritoryKey = st.SalesTerritoryKey
GROUP BY st.SalesTerritoryRegion, st.SalesTerritoryCountry
ORDER BY AvgOrderValue DESC
description: Aggregation with calculated average order value by territory

natural_language: Show me total reseller sales by product category
sql:
SELECT
    pc.EnglishProductCategoryName AS Category,
    SUM(frs.SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    SUM(frs.OrderQuantity) AS TotalUnitsSold,
    COUNT(DISTINCT frs.SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactResellerSales frs
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON frs.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductSubcategory ps ON p.ProductSubcategoryKey = ps.ProductSubcategoryKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProductCategory pc ON ps.ProductCategoryKey = pc.ProductCategoryKey
GROUP BY pc.EnglishProductCategoryName
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
description: Reseller sales aggregated by product category

natural_language: Compare reseller vs internet sales totals
sql:
SELECT
    'Reseller' AS SalesChannel,
    SUM(SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactResellerSales
UNION ALL
SELECT
    'Internet' AS SalesChannel,
    SUM(SalesAmount) AS TotalRevenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT SalesOrderNumber) AS OrderCount
FROM dbo.FactInternetSales
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
description: UNION query comparing two fact tables

natural_language: What is the current inventory level by product?
sql:
SELECT
    p.EnglishProductName,
    p.ProductLine,
    SUM(fpi.UnitsBalance) AS CurrentInventory,
    AVG(fpi.UnitsBalance) AS AvgInventoryLevel
FROM dbo.FactProductInventory fpi
INNER JOIN dbo.DimProduct p ON fpi.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
WHERE fpi.MovementDate = (SELECT MAX(MovementDate) FROM dbo.FactProductInventory)
GROUP BY p.EnglishProductName, p.ProductLine, p.ProductKey
ORDER BY CurrentInventory DESC
description: Inventory snapshot query with subquery for latest date

natural_language: Show me sales quotas by employee and year
sql:
SELECT
    e.FirstName + ' ' + e.LastName AS EmployeeName,
    e.Title,
    d.CalendarYear,
    d.CalendarQuarter,
    SUM(fsq.SalesAmountQuota) AS QuarterlyQuota
FROM dbo.FactSalesQuota fsq
INNER JOIN dbo.DimEmployee e ON fsq.EmployeeKey = e.EmployeeKey
INNER JOIN dbo.DimDate d ON fsq.DateKey = d.DateKey
GROUP BY e.FirstName, e.LastName, e.Title, d.CalendarYear, d.CalendarQuarter
ORDER BY d.CalendarYear, d.CalendarQuarter, QuarterlyQuota DESC
description: Sales quota analysis by employee (note: treats quota data as non-sensitive for learning)
